THE DEMURRER TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED BY SAFEWAY SERVICES, LLC IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. SAFWAY HAS LEAVE TO CORRECT THIS DEFICIENCY IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Successor Agency To The Redevelopment Agency Of The City And County Of San Francisco, Commonly Known As Office Of Community Investment And Infrastructure For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-22-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Ralph R. Rhoades, Esq. of PARTON & SELL, T:415.258.9700, for plaintiff/moving party;
Dennis Avilucea, Jr., Esq. of BORTON PETRINI LLP, T:408.535.0870, for defendants/opposing party;
Helen Greenberg Esq. of SEVERSON & WERSON APC, T:415.398.3344, for cross complainant, on Court Call;
Elaine Tanudjaja, Esq. of FRIEDENTHAL, HEFFERNAN & BROWN LLP, T:626.628.2800, for defendants, on Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF SUCCESSOR AGENCY TO THE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY Of The City And County Of San Francisco, Commonly Known As Office Of Community Investment And Infrastructure's Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint, as follows:

The motion of Plaintiff Successor Agency to the Redevelopment Agency of the City and County of San Francisco, commonly known as Office of Community Investment and Infrastructure, for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. California's strong policy favoring amendments to pleadings supports the motion. Defendant San Francisco Museum of Modern Art has not shown that it will be prejudiced by the amendment. Plaintiff has offered to stipulate to shortened notice on any pleading or summary judgment motion Defendant wishes to bring before trial. If Defendant needs additional time to complete discovery, it may seek a continuance of the trial date in Dept. 206.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
